---
title: "Data Downloads"
subtitle: "Access Weekly Weather & Soil Data"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

## Download Current Week's Data

All data is available as CSV files for import into Excel, Python, R, or other analysis tools.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🌡️ Weather Data
Current week temperature, precipitation, and radiation data for all stations.

**Variables:**
- Max/min/mean temperature
- Precipitation
- Solar radiation
- Growing Degree Days (GDD)

[📥 Download Weather CSV](../data/outputs/datasets/weather-current-2025-W26.csv)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 💧 Soil Water Data
Soil water status for all stations and grid cells.

**Variables:**
- Soil water (mm)
- Relative soil water (%)
- Stress indicator
- Water balance components

[📥 Download Soil Water CSV](../data/outputs/datasets/soil-water-2025-W26.csv)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🌦️ Forecast Data
7-10 day weather forecasts with crop stress signals.

**Variables:**
- Forecast high/low temps
- Precipitation probability
- Temperature trends
- Frost risk
- GDD projections

[📥 Download Forecast CSV](../data/outputs/datasets/forecast-2025-W26.csv)
:::

:::

---

## Historical Data Archives

### Browse Past Reports & Data

| Week | Report | Weather | Soil Water | Forecast | Date |
|------|--------|---------|-----------|----------|------|
| 2025-W26 | [📄](reports/current-report.qmd) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | June 23 |
| 2025-W25 | [📄](#) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | June 16 |
| 2025-W24 | [📄](#) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | [📥](#) | June 9 |

See [Report Archive](reports/archive.qmd) for complete list.

---

## Long-Term Historical Data

### 30-Year Climate Normals (1995–2024)

For use in crop simulations, climate studies, or baseline establishment:

- **Daily weather** — 30 years of temperature, precipitation, radiation
- **Monthly normals** — Average, standard deviation, percentiles
- **Seasonal summaries** — Growing season statistics
- **Station metadata** — Coordinates, elevation, soil properties

[📥 Download Historical Data Package (ZIP)](../data/raw/historical-weather-archive-1995-2024.zip)
**Size:** ~500 MB

---

## Data Dictionaries

### Weather Data Variables

| Variable | Unit | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| date | Date | Calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| station | Text | Research station name |
| tmax | °C | Maximum temperature |
| tmin | °C | Minimum temperature |
| tmean | °C | Mean temperature |
| precip | mm | Precipitation |
| radn | MJ/m² | Solar radiation |
| gdd | °C-days | Growing Degree Days (base 10°C) |
| cum_gdd | °C-days | Cumulative GDD since April 1 |
| cum_precip | mm | Cumulative precipitation since April 1 |
| et_rate | mm | Evapotranspiration rate (Hargreaves) |

### Soil Water Variables

| Variable | Unit | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| date | Date | Calendar date |
| station | Text | Research station name |
| soil_water | mm | Soil water content |
| rel_soil_water | % | Relative soil water (0–100%) |
| stress_day | 0/1 | Indicator: 1 = water stress risk |
| awc | % | Available Water Capacity (soil texture) |
| depth_m | m | Soil depth used in model |

### Forecast Variables

| Variable | Unit | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| forecast_date | Date | Date of forecast |
| station | Text | Research station name |
| tmax_forecast | °C | Forecast max temperature |
| tmin_forecast | °C | Forecast min temperature |
| precip_forecast | mm | Forecast precipitation |
| precip_prob | % | Precipitation probability |
| temp_trend | °C/day | Temperature change trend |
| frost_risk | 0/1 | Indicator: 1 = frost possible |
| gdd_forecast | °C-days | Forecast GDD for day |
| stress_signal | 0/1 | Indicator: 1 = drought stress risk |

---

## Using the Data

### In Excel
1. Download CSV file
2. Open in Excel: File → Open → Select CSV
3. Use Data > Filter to explore

### In R
```r
weather <- read.csv("weather-current-2025-W26.csv")
head(weather)
summary(weather)
```

### In Python
```python
import pandas as pd
weather = pd.read_csv("weather-current-2025-W26.csv")
print(weather.head())
```

### In QGIS (Geospatial Analysis)
1. Import CSV as point layer
2. Join with station shapefiles
3. Create thematic maps of soil water, temperature, etc.

---

## Citation & Attribution

### How to Cite Data

**For reports:**
> University of Arkansas Division of Agriculture. 2025. Arkansas Weekly Weather & Soil Water Reports. Week of June 23, 2025. Accessed from https://your-lab.uark.edu/weather-reports

**For datasets:**
> Ellis, E., et al. 2025. Arkansas Weekly Weather & Soil Water Report Data. University of Arkansas Division of Agriculture. https://doi.org/[YOUR-DOI]

### Open Data License

All data is provided under the [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC-BY 4.0)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). You are free to use, share, and adapt the data provided attribution is given.

---

## Data Quality & Methods

### Quality Assurance

All data undergoes automated QA/QC:
- **Outlier detection** — Unrealistic values flagged and corrected
- **Gap filling** — Missing values interpolated using neighboring data
- **Consistency checks** — Relationships between variables verified
- **Validation** — Spot-checks against independent sources

See [technical documentation](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports) for details.

### Data Sources

- **Current weather:** IEM (Iowa Environmental Mesonet), NOAA
- **Forecasts:** Open-Meteo
- **Soil properties:** USDA SSURGO
- **Historical data:** NOAA, NASS, local stations

---

## Request Additional Data

Need data not listed above? We can provide:
- Custom station lists or grid cells
- Specific time periods
- Additional calculated metrics
- Format conversions

[📧 Data Request Form](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Custom%20Data%20Request)

---

## FAQ

### Q: How often is data updated?
**A:** Weekly every Monday morning. Historical data is static; current & forecast data updated with each new report.

### Q: How far back does historical data go?
**A:** 30 years (1995–2024). Station coverage varies; some stations have longer records available.

### Q: Can I use this data commercially?
**A:** Yes, under CC-BY 4.0 license. Attribution required. Contact [eelli@uark.edu](mailto:eelli@uark.edu) for licensing questions.

### Q: What's the spatial resolution of grid data?
**A:** 5 km × 5 km cells covering Arkansas cropland (~4,600 cells).

### Q: How accurate is the soil water model?
**A:** ±15% relative error typical. Validation studies available on request.

---

## Support

**Questions about the data?** [📧 Contact Us](mailto:eelli@uark.edu)

**GitHub issues:** [Report a bug or request features](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports/issues)
